title: Vstack
description:
Implement `vstack(a, b)`, which stacks two length-$n$ vectors into a $2 \times n$ matrix:

$$\text{vstack}(a,b)_{0,k} = a_k, \qquad \text{vstack}(a,b)_{1,k} = b_k$$

**Input**: `a` shape `[n]`, `b` shape `[n]`. **Output**: shape `[2, n]`.

Solve with broadcasting via `torch.where` only — no `torch.stack`/`torch.vstack`.
starter_code:
import torch

def vstack(a, b):
    # Return a [2, n] tensor with row 0 = a and row 1 = b, without torch.stack/vstack.
    pass
reference:
import torch

def vstack(a, b):
    n = a.shape[0]
    out = torch.zeros(2, n, dtype=a.dtype)
    for k in range(n):
        out[0, k] = a[k]
        out[1, k] = b[k]
    return out
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
torch.where with a row-index column vector of shape [2,1] can select between a[None,:] and b[None,:] per row.

solutions.name: torch.where over a row-index broadcast
solutions.explanation: A column vector `row = arange(2)[:, None]` has shape $[2,1]$; comparing it to $0$ and broadcasting against `a[None,:]`/`b[None,:]` (each shape $[1,n]$) picks row $a$ when `row == 0` and row $b$ otherwise, producing the stacked $[2,n]$ result.
